frames.title: Start with a plain-language prompt
frames.desc: Describe the business app you want, and let the wizard propose the first draft of the specification.

frames.title: Refine the generated specification
frames.desc: The AI asks clarifying questions and expands the app definition. You answer in plain language: respond to those questions, or add other notes and requirements whenever you need to.

frames.title: Generate the data model, then tweak it
frames.desc: Move from specification to ERD. You stay on the diagram and iterate as long as you need: refine with AI, or edit the model manually and have AI review. Clarifying questions appear along the way, and you can answer those or add other requirements in plain language.

frames.title: Set theme, spacing, and visual style
frames.desc: Pick colors, layout rules, typography, and component styling to shape the application experience. All of it can be revised after the initial build.

frames.title: Brand the application and organize navigation
frames.desc: Name the app, set logos, choose the home page, and fine-tune the menu structure before publishing.

frames.title: Publish and open the generated app
frames.desc: Once the build is complete, the portal is ready immediately — no handoff, no separate deployment step.

frames.title: Users enter the finished portal
frames.desc: What started as a prompt becomes a real business application with branded entry, navigation, and workflows.

frames.title: Create records with guided forms
frames.desc: The portal is not a mockup. It is the actual application the team can start using right away.

frames.title: End on the live business view
frames.desc: Close on the finished CRM list screen to reinforce the outcome: fast creation, human refinement, real operations.
